fixes_applied: tests/test.sh: extended file locator to also accept packages/coding-agent/examples/extensions/*.ts (the canonical-patch path); previously only .pi/extensions/*.ts was scanned, which is the runtime install path the instruction mandates but does NOT match where the upstream patch actually places the file.
tests/test.sh: extended both upstream f2p gates (f2p_upstream_file_exists, f2p_upstream_ext_loadable) to search both candidate paths; first match wins, gate detail reports which path was used.

tests/test.sh: harness ctxMock now exposes ctx.isIdle() returning false. The canonical handler calls ctx.isIdle() in the /start path; without this it would throw and the activation handler would abort before pi.sendMessage(...) ran.

tests/test.sh: harness piMock Proxy now intercepts pi.sendMessage(m, opts) and pushes m onto sessionMessages, so f2p_gate2_protocol_injected can see the protocol instructions when the extension uses pi.sendMessage (canonical) rather than ctx.addMessage. SESSION_HINTS regex picks up the [[PI_SIGNAL_OPEN_UI]] etc. tokens inside the captured message content.

validation_results.reasoning: Predicted analytically (no docker run available in this curator environment). Nop baseline: file locator finds no new .ts in either search dir; finalize() exits with REWARD=0; trap upstream gates also FAIL (no UPSTREAM_FILE) so f2p_any_pass=False and existing<=0, reward stays 0.0. Canonical patch: writes packages/coding-agent/examples/extensions/message-signal.ts (now picked up by the extended search) with pi.registerCommand('start',...), pi.on('message_end',...), pi.sendMessage(protocol-with-[[PI_SIGNAL_*]] markers). With the harness fixes: G1 PASS (cmd + message_end handler), G2 PASS (sendMessage captured to sessionMessages, regex matches PI_SIGNAL_OPEN_UI), G3 PASS (notify+setWidget on signals → reactions ≥1), G4 PASS (open+close+done all distinct), G5 PASS (enabled=false initial → no passive reaction), G6 PASS ([[PI_...]] pattern present, no registerTool). Inner=1.00; upstream f2p_file_exists+ext_loadable PASS (0.10). Final reward = 1.00*0.90 + 0.10 = 1.00.

rescue_reasoning: Prior DROP cited two issues: (1) verifier-vs-canonical path mismatch (.pi/extensions/ vs packages/coding-agent/examples/extensions/) and (2) upstream maintainer rejected the example. Issue (1) is purely mechanical and now fixed: verifier accepts both locations. Issue (2) is a category error — the benchmark scores agent deliverables against a behavioral verifier, not whether upstream merged. The session itself is internally consistent: agent built the file, user said 'move that to cwd/.pi/extensions so i can relaod', agent moved it, user iterated on signal semantics for 10+ turns. The instruction.md asks for a working demonstration of the signal pattern; the verifier checks behavior (command registration, message_end handler, signal detection, distinct open/close kinds, inertness when inactive, no tool registration). All implementation-agnostic. The canonical patch satisfies all 6 behavioral gates after the harness mock fixes. T0 ceiling is meaningful: the 6 gates discriminate via Gate 4 (multiple signal kinds) and Gate 5 (passive-when-inactive), neither of which a stub solution would satisfy. Multi-turn signal evolved through the session (open vs close vs done; turn-based test) so the user-sim has plenty of correction surface.
notes: Path-mismatch was real but salvageable; the full DROP cascade (rejected upstream → no canon → drop) was over-conservative. The verifier itself was already well-designed (6 behavioral gates, no narrow string matching, accepts both message_end and message_update handlers, accepts notify OR setWidget OR events.emit as 'reaction'). The two harness gaps (missing ctx.isIdle and unintercepted pi.sendMessage) were silent bugs that would have failed the canonical even if the path search had been correct from day one. Both fixes are non-invasive: they only ADD behaviors to the mock, never remove or constrain. Image rebuild not required (test.sh is mounted at runtime, not baked in).
